Name: Pashupatinath Temple
History: Founded 5th century AD; current pagoda rebuilt 17th century; UNESCO WHS 1979.

Name: Boudhanath Stupa
History: Built 14th century; major Tibetan Buddhist centre; UNESCO WHS 1979.

Name: Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple)
History: Over 2,000 years old; self-manifested stupa; UNESCO WHS 1979.

Name: Kathmandu Durbar Square
History: Malla & Shah dynasty palaces; built 12th–18th century; UNESCO WHS.

Name: Patan Durbar Square
History: Capital of Lalitpur under Malla kings; UNESCO WHS; home of Krishna Mandir.

Name: Bhaktapur Durbar Square
History: Founded 9th century; best-preserved medieval square; UNESCO WHS.

Name: Pokhara Lakeside
History: Developed as tourist hub after 1970s; gateway to Annapurna treks.

Name: Phewa Lake
History: Sacred to goddess Tal Barahi; Nepal's 2nd largest lake; famous for Machhapuchhre reflection.

Name: Annapurna Base Camp
History: Opened to trekkers 1977; 360° Himalayan views at 4,130m.

Name: Everest Base Camp
History: Everest first summited May 29, 1953 by Tenzing & Hillary; opened to trekkers 1960s.

Name: Chitwan National Park
History: Nepal's first NP 1973; royal hunting grounds before conservation; UNESCO WHS 1984.

Name: Lumbini
History: Birthplace of Lord Buddha c. 623 BC; Ashoka Pillar erected 249 BC; UNESCO WHS 1997.

Name: Bandipur
History: Prosperous 18th-19th century Newari trading town; government-protected settlement zone.

Name: Nagarkot
History: Historic royal retreat at 2,195m; panoramic views of 8 Himalayan ranges including Everest.

Name: Gosaikunda Lake
History: Sacred alpine lake at 4,380m; mentioned in Hindu scriptures; linked to Lord Shiva.

Name: Muktinath Temple
History: Sacred at 3,800m for Hindus & Buddhists; mentioned in Mahabharata; 108 water spouts.

Name: Rara Lake
History: Nepal's largest lake at 2,990m; Rara NP established 1975; entire village relocated for conservation.

Name: Ilam Tea Gardens
History: Tea cultivation introduced 1873; largest tea estate in Nepal; premier agri-tourism destination.

Name: Upper Mustang (Lo Manthang)
History: Former Kingdom of Lo; closed to outsiders until 1992; Tibetan culture and cave monasteries.

Name: Poon Hill
History: Sunrise viewpoint at 3,210m; panoramic Dhaulagiri & Annapurna views; famous short trek stop.
